briefdescription.para: TX Checksum offloading supported for all of IPv4, UDP, TCP.
RX Checksum offloading supported for all of IPv4, UDP, TCP.
VLAN supported.
10 Mbits link supported
100 Mbits link supported
1 Gbits link supported
2.5 Gbits link supported
5 Gbits link supported
IEEE 802.1AS (gPTP) clock supported.
IEEE 802.1Qav (credit-based shaping) supported.
Promiscuous mode supported.
Priority queues available.
MAC address filtering supported.
Link Layer Discovery Protocol supported.
VLAN Tag stripping.
DSA switch user port.
DSA switch conduit port.
IEEE 802.1Qbv (scheduled traffic) supported.
IEEE 802.1Qbu (frame preemption) supported.
TXTIME supported.
TX-Injection supported.
Ethernet hardware capabilities.
IEEE 802.3 Ethernet (default)
IEEE 802.11 Wi-Fi.
Types of Ethernet L2.
Device does not support any L3/L4 checksum offloading.
Device supports checksum offloading for the IPv4 header.
Device supports checksum offloading for ICMPv4 payload (implies IPv4 header)
Device supports checksum offloading for the IPv6 header.
Device supports checksum offloading for ICMPv6 payload (implies IPv6 header)
Device supports TCP checksum offloading for all supported IP protocols.
Device supports UDP checksum offloading for all supported IP protocols.
Protocols that are supported by checksum offloading.
Common statistics only (excludes vendor statistics)
Vendor statistics only.
All statistics.
Ethernet statistics type (bitmap)
MAC address is handled by the driver (backwards compatible case)
A random MAC address is generated during initialization.
The MAC address is read from an NVMEM cell.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_NVMEM.

briefdescription.para: A static MAC address is provided in the device tree.
MAC address configuration types.
Check if the Ethernet MAC address is a broadcast address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is a broadcast address, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is a all zeroes address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to an Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is an all zeroes address, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is unspecified.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is unspecified, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is a multicast address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is a multicast address, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is an unicast address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is an unicast address, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is valid.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is valid, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is a LLDP multicast address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is a LLDP multicast address, false if not

briefdescription.para: Check if the Ethernet MAC address is a PTP multicast address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer to a Ethernet MAC address.

simplesect.para: true if address is a PTP multicast address, false if not

briefdescription.para: Return Ethernet broadcast address.

simplesect.para: Ethernet broadcast address.

briefdescription.para: Convert IPv4 multicast address to Ethernet address.
Convert IPv6 multicast address to Ethernet address.
Return ethernet device hardware capability information.

simplesect.para: Hardware capabilities

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, enum ethernet_config_type type, struct ethernet_config *config)
memberdef.name: net_eth_get_hw_config

briefdescription.para: Return ethernet device hardware configuration information.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Add VLAN tag to the interface.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Remove VLAN tag from the interface.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Return VLAN tag specified to network interface.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the interface parameter must be the VLAN interface, and not the Ethernet one.

simplesect.para: VLAN tag for this interface or NET_VLAN_TAG_UNSPEC if VLAN is not configured for that interface.

briefdescription.para: Return network interface related to this VLAN tag.

parameterdescription.para: Main network interface (not the VLAN one).

simplesect.para: Network interface related to this tag or NULL if no such interface exists.

briefdescription.para: Return main network interface that is attached to this VLAN tag.

parameterdescription.para: VLAN network interface. This is used to get the pointer to ethernet L2 context

simplesect.para: Network interface related to this tag or NULL if no such interface exists.

briefdescription.para: Check if there are any VLAN interfaces enabled to this specific Ethernet network interface.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the iface must be the actual Ethernet interface and not the virtual VLAN interface.

simplesect.para: True if there are enabled VLANs for this network interface, false if not.

briefdescription.para: Get VLAN status for a given network interface (enabled or not).

simplesect.para: True if VLAN is enabled for this network interface, false if not.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given interface is a VLAN interface.

simplesect.para: True if this network interface is VLAN one, false if not.

briefdescription.para: Load a MAC address from a MAC address configuration structure with the specified type.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of a randomized MAC address, the universal vs local (U/L) bit is set to a locally administered address (LAA) and the unicast vs multicast (I/G) bit is cleared to make it a unicast address.

parameterdescription.para: The resulting MAC address buffer, needs a size of NET_ETH_ADDR_LEN bytes.
No MAC address configuration data is loaded.

briefdescription.para: Inform ethernet L2 driver that ethernet carrier is detected.

detaileddescription.para: This happens when cable is connected.

briefdescription.para: Inform ethernet L2 driver that ethernet carrier was lost.

detaileddescription.para: This happens when cable is disconnected.

briefdescription.para: Set promiscuous mode either ON or OFF.

simplesect.para: 0 if mode set or unset was successful, <0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set TX-Injection mode either ON or OFF.

simplesect.para: 0 if mode set or unset was successful, <0 otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, struct net_eth_addr *mac, enum ethernet_filter_type type, bool enable)
memberdef.name: net_eth_mac_filter

briefdescription.para: Set or unset HW filtering for MAC address mac.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an ethernet MAC address
Filter type, either source or destination

simplesect.para: 0 if filter set or unset was successful, <0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Return the PHY device that is tied to this ethernet network interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to PHY device if found, NULL if not found.

briefdescription.para: Return PTP clock that is tied to this ethernet network interface.

simplesect.para: Pointer to PTP clock if found, NULL if not found or if this ethernet interface does not support PTP.

briefdescription.para: Return PTP clock that is tied to this ethernet network interface index.

simplesect.para: Pointer to PTP clock if found, NULL if not found or if this ethernet interface index does not support PTP.

briefdescription.para: Return PTP port number attached to this interface.

simplesect.para: Port number, no such port if < 0

briefdescription.para: Set PTP port number attached to this interface.
Check if the Ethernet L2 network interface can perform Wi-Fi.

simplesect.para: True if interface supports Wi-Fi, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Ethernet MAC address length.
Minimum Ethernet frame size.
Ethernet MTU size.

memberdef.name: ETH_NET_DEVICE_INIT
memberdef.initializer: Z_ETH_NET_DEVICE_INIT(DT_INVALID_NODE, dev_id, name, init_fn, \ pm, data, config, prio, api, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Create an Ethernet network interface and bind it to network device.

parameterdescription.para: The name this instance of the driver exposes to the system.
Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

memberdef.name: ETH_NET_DEVICE_INIT_INSTANCE
memberdef.initializer: Z_ETH_NET_DEVICE_INIT_INSTANCE(DT_INVALID_NODE, dev_id, name, \ instance, init_fn, pm, data, \ config, prio, api, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Create multiple Ethernet network interfaces and bind them to network devices.

detaileddescription.para: If your network device needs more than one instance of a network interface, use this macro below and provide a different instance suffix each time (0, 1, 2, ... or a, b, c ... whatever works for you)

parameterdescription.para: The name this instance of the driver exposes to the system.
Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

memberdef.name: ETH_NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: Z_ETH_NET_DEVICE_INIT(node_id, Z_DEVICE_DT_DEV_ID(node_id), \ DEVICE_DT_NAME(node_id), init_fn, pm, \ data, config, prio, api, mtu)

briefdescription.para: Like ETH_NET_DEVICE_INIT but taking metadata from a devicetree.

detaileddescription.para: Create an Ethernet network interface and bind it to network device.

parameterdescription.para: Address to the init function of the driver.
Reference to struct pm_device associated with the device. (optional).
Pointer to the device's private data.
The address to the structure containing the configuration information for this instance of the driver.
The initialization level at which configuration occurs.
Provides an initial pointer to the API function struct used by the driver. Can be NULL.
Maximum transfer unit in bytes for this network interface.

briefdescription.para: Like ETH_NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.

parameterdescription.para: instance number. This is replaced by DT_DRV_COMPAT(inst) in the call to ETH_NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE.
other parameters as expected by ETH_NET_DEVICE_DT_DEFINE.

briefdescription.para: Ethernet L3 protocol register macro.

parameterdescription.para: Handler function for this protocol type.

memberdef.name: NET_ETH_MAC_DT_CONFIG_INIT
memberdef.initializer: COND_CASE_1(DT_PROP(node_id, zephyr_random_mac_address), \ (Z_NET_ETH_MAC_DT_CONFIG_INIT_RANDOM(node_id)), \ DT_NVMEM_CELLS_HAS_NAME(node_id, mac_address), \ (Z_NET_ETH_MAC_DT_CONFIG_INIT_NVMEM(node_id)), \ DT_NODE_HAS_PROP(node_id, local_mac_address), \ (Z_NET_ETH_MAC_DT_CONFIG_INIT_STATIC(node_id)), \ (Z_NET_ETH_MAC_DT_CONFIG_INIT_DEFAULT(node_id)))

briefdescription.para: Initialize the ethernet MAC config for the given DT node identifier.
Like NET_ETH_MAC_DT_CONFIG_INIT for an instance of a DT_DRV_COMPAT compatible.
Ethernet support functions.
